meaning: v.(使)烦恼,(使)焦急,(使)腐蚀,(使)磨损
examples: "You really needn't fret, Ratty," added the Badger placidly.
Her baby starts to fret as soon as she goes out of the room.
But congressional staffers fret that the project will eventually cost billions more.
Even people in the snoop business fret that their gear could be taken too far.
Besides, they fret, why assume that county jails will be any better than state prisons?
